title: trapa natans var. borysthenica
intro: Trapa natans var. borysthenica, also known as the European water chestnut or Borysthenica water chestnut, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Trapaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote urination. Trapa natans var. borysthenica is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or taken as a supplement.
